fault_hypothesis: The function incorrectly removes the last element if it is common to both input lists. The conditional logic on line 74 is faulty, causing the `pop()` on line 75 to be executed erroneously when it should not.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: This special de-duplication logic for the final element should never trigger in a correct merge.
After merging the main parts and tails, the list must contain all elements from both inputs.
The branch that removes a supposedly duplicate element should never be executed.
The final returned list must have a length equal to the sum of the input list lengths.
